data.title: JinkoSolar upsizes and extends share repurchase program
data.description:
• JinkoSolar (NYSE:JKS) said that its board of directors approved the upsizing and extension of its existing share repurchase program.

  • On December 10, 2024, the upsized the existing share repurchase program from $200M to $350M.

  • In addition, the Board has also approved to extend the existing share repurchase program for an additional 12-month period through June 30, 2026.

  • Under the extended share repurchase program, the company may repurchase up to ~$215.5M of its Class A ordinary shares represented by the ADSs through June 30, 2026.

  • "The upsizing and extension of the Existing Share Repurchase Program further reflect our continued confidence in our long-term business growth." said Mr. Xiande Li, JinkoSolar's Chairman and CEO.

  • The stock price dropped 1.3% on Tuesday during after market hours of trading.

data.title: Fluence Energy upsizes and prices $350M senior convertible notes
data.description:
• Fluence Energy (NASDAQ:FLNC) upsized and priced an offering of $350M 2.25% convertible senior notes due 2030.

  • The offering size was increased from the previously announced offering size of $300M notes.

  • The issuance and sale of the Notes are scheduled to settle on December 12, 2024.

  • Initial purchasers have an option to purchase, for settlement within a period of 13 days from, and including, the date the Notes are first issued, up to an additional $50M notes.

  • Net proceeds will be used for working capital needs, upgrading one of our battery cell production lines and general corporate purposes.

data.title: Ventas declares $0.45 dividend
data.description:
• Ventas (NYSE:VTR) declares $0.45/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.94%
  • Payable Jan. 16; for shareholders of record Dec. 31; ex-div Dec. 31.
  • See VTR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Coastal Financial prices $85M stock offering at $71 per share
data.description:
• Coastal Financial (NASDAQ:CCB) priced an underwritten public offering of 1.2M shares at a price to the public of $71.00 per share.

  • Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 180,000 shares.

  • The aggregate gross proceeds of the offering will be approximately $85.2M and $98M in case underwriters’ option to purchase additional shares is exercised in full.

  • Proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, including, without limitation, supporting investment opportunities and the Bank’s growth.

  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 12, 2024.

  • The stock price marginally dropped 0.86% on Tuesday during after market hours of trading.

data.title: Wednesday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
7:00 AM MBA Mortgage Applications

The data point measures the change in the number of new applications for mortgages backed by the MBA during the reported week. Mortgage applications rose for the consecutive fourth time to 2.8 for the week ending December 4. The benchmark 30-year fixed mortgage rate dipped 17 bps to 6.69%.

8:30 AM Consumer Price Index

The Consumer Price Index measures the change in the price of goods and services from the perspective of the consumer. It is a key way to measure changes in purchasing trends and inflation. November CPI is expected to edge higher to 2.7% from 2.6% in October. Core CPI, which strips food and energy is expected to be held steady at 3.3%.

10:30 AM EIA Petroleum Inventories

The Energy Information Administration weekly data reports inventory levels of US crude oil, gasoline and distillates stocks. The figure shows how much oil and products are available in storage. U.S. commercial crude oil inventories decreased by 5.07 million barrels in the last week.

1:00 PM 10-Yr Note Auction

The 10-year Treasury yield is the interest rate the U.S. government pays investors who purchase 10-year Treasury notes, also known as T-notes. The treasury will hold an auction of $39 Billion, 10-year note. The yield on the US 10-year Treasury remained below 4.15% on Monday, hovering at seven-week lows as investors awaited crucial inflation data this week, which could influence the Federal Reserve's upcoming interest rate decision.

2:00 PM Treasury Statement

The Monthly Treasury Statement summarizes the financial activities of the federal government and off-budget federal entities and conforms to the Budget of the U.S. Government. The MTS presents a summary of: Receipts and outlays. Surplus or deficit.

Click here to see the weekly economic calendar.

data.title: Bausch & Lomb falls amid report Blackstone may drop out of takeover bid
data.description:
Bausch & Lomb (NYSE:BLCO) dropped 9% in after hours trading after a report that private equity firm Blackstone (BX) may drop out of a group bidding for the eyecare company.

Blackstone (BX) may no longer be interested in being a part of a joint bid with PE firm TPG Inc. (TPG) for Bausch & Lomb (BLCO) due to the seller's high price expectations, according to a Financial Times report on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Blackstone (BX) will likely exit the consortium, which reduces the odds of deal getting done, according to the report. Bausch + Lomb’s (BLCO) board wanted an offer at a sizeable premium to $20 a share in a deal, but the consortium of TPG and Blackstone appeared to be averse to such a high price, according to the report.

Blackstone, TPG and Bausch + Lomb declined to comment to the FT.

The latest update comes after the Financial Times reported in October that TPG (TPG) and Blackstone (BX) are working on a joint bid for the eyecare company. The newspaper first reported that the eyecare firm was exploring a sale to address concerns raised by lenders such as Apollo Global Management (APO) over its planned separation from its parent, Bausch Health (BHC).

data.title: Auto disruption: Amazon joins Costco as a source for consumers to buy cars
data.description:
Amazon Auto officially launched in 48 U.S. cities on Tuesday as Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) looks to compete with Costco's (COST) popular car buying program. Initially, Amazon Autos will offer online automotive shopping from Hyundai (OTCPK:HYMTF) dealers across major metropolitan areas nationwide. In 2025, Amazon (AMZN) plans to roll out additional manufacturers, brands, cities, and new functionality for the online car buying program.

The Amazon Autos online experience will let browse and search for available vehicles at their local dealer by make, model, trim, color, and features. Customers can also get an instant valuation and trade in their existing vehicle toward the price of the new car. The program was also noted to feature transparent pricing and allow options for financing.

"We're partnering with dealers and brands to redesign car shopping—making it more transparent, convenient, and customer-friendly," stated Amazon Auto top executive Fan Jin.

Auto dealer decline?

Costco's automotive program allows members to purchase both new and certified pre-owned vehicles. The program partners with a network of over 3,000 dealerships across the U.S. Meanwhile, Walmart's (WMT) car buying service is less established than Costco's (COST). It primarily acts as a facilitator, directing customers to local dealerships without a structured program like Costco's.

The aggressive approach by Amazon (AMZN), Costco (COST), and Walmart (WMT) to sell autos to consumers is of high interest to major auto manufacturers and auto retailers such as Carvana (CVNA), CarMax (KMX), Lithia Motors (LAD), AutoNation (AN), Asbury Automotive Group (ABG), CarGurus (CARG), Group 1 Automotive (GPI), Sonic Automotive (SAH), Penske Automotive Group (PAG), and Cars.com (CARS).

data.title: Investors moving to natural gas, away from oil as 2025 energy stock play
data.description:
Crude oil futures ended fractionally higher Tuesday, with the market weighing the Middle East situation after the fall of the Assad regime in Syria, expectations of Chinese monetary stimulus and the path of U.S. interest rates.

While Syria is not a major oil producer, it is strategically located and has had strong ties with Russia and Iran.

The Middle East actually could become "less influential" for oil prices after Assad's downfall, XS.com analyst Samer Hasn said, with the "potential diminishing of the risk premium around disruptions to crude flows in the region."

"Syria was the most important corridor to bypass U.S. sanctions and a destination for Iranian oil exports," Hasn wrote, so "this structural shift may make Iran less willing to escalate in the region, which may entail risks to the safety of crude flows, which are now tighter for Iran."

Wednesday's OPEC monthly report "could offer additional guidance that could tilt bearish if the organization opts to adjust oil demand lower toward the IEA's forecast that has been offering an entirely different picture of global oil demand going forward," Ritterbusch analysts said, adding that "the geopolitical factor that has been spiking oil values occasionally during the past couple of years is beginning to wear thin as a pricing influence."

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for January delivery closed +0.3% at $68.59/bbl, and front-month February Brent (CO1:COM) ended +0.1% at $72.19/bbl, while January Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) settled -0.6% to $3.163/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (NYSEARCA:UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Oil names are out and gas names are in among energy investors, as traders increasingly bet on gains in gas to fuel their portfolios more than crude, Bloomberg reports.

The First Trust Natural Gas ETF (FCG) has enjoyed nine straight weeks of fund inflows, the longest streak since 2021; Comstock Resources (CRK) is the fund's top performer during the period, jumping 22%, and EQT (EQT) and Gulfport Energy (GPOR) have gained more than 15% over the same period.

J.P. Morgan analyst Arun Jayaram predicts a "role reversal" next year, in which investors should favor natural gas names over oil stocks, given expectations for an oversupplied oil market and “tailwinds” supporting the gas producers such as a boost in LNG exports from North America and rising demand for gas-fired power demand by data centers.

J.P. Morgan's favorite natgas producers to play the theme are Antero Resources (AR), EQT (EQT) and Gulfport (GPOR).

data.title: Gold's long-term upside outlook still intact, Citi says
data.description:
Gold futures jumped Tuesday to the highest close in more than two weeks, after China's central bank restarted gold purchases for the first time in six months and the Chinese government pledged to adopt an "appropriately loose" monetary policy and a more proactive fiscal approach next year.

The People's Bank of China bought around five metric tons of gold in November, a relatively small amount compared with previous purchases of up to 30 tons/month, but the resumption of purchasing at all is the key factor in gold's latest rally, Commerzbank analysts said.

The buying may be in response to Donald Trump's election victory, which threatens China with the potential introduction of punitive tariffs, Commerzbank said, also noting the price of gold had dropped significantly after a series of records, which may have stimulated buying interest.

For gold to add to gains, central bank purchases would need to continue in the coming months, the bank said.

Also, expectations have solidified among traders that the Federal Reserve will cut U.S. interest rates later this month, enhancing the appeal of non-interest bearing bullion, according to ActivTrades analyst Ricardo Evangelista.

The spotlight will move to the U.S. Consumer Price Index on Wednesday and the Producer Price Index on Thursday, both considered key in shaping the Federal Reserve's rate-cut decisions.

With two U.S. rate cuts so far this year, traders predict an 86% chance of another 25-bp cut at the Fed's meeting next week, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for December delivery ended +1.2% to $2,697.60/oz, its best settlement value since November 22, and front-month December silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +0.4% to $32.363/oz, the highest since November 5.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Gold's long-term upside remains intact, Citi analysts said, supported by ongoing deterioration of the U.S. labor market, still-elevated interest rates weighing on growth, and higher demand for ETFs.

Long-term themes including rising global debt levels amid continued "de-dollarization" also are structurally positive for gold, Citi said, maintaining price targets of $2,800/oz for up to three months and $3,000/oz for 6-12 months.

data.title: Figs jumps after report of $1B takeover offer from PE firm
data.description:
Medical-apparel maker Figs (NYSE:FIGS) soared 9.2% in after hours trading on a report that private-equity firm Story3 Capital Partners made a more than $1 billion takeover offer.

Story3 offered $6 a share for Figs (FIGS), according to a WSJ report on Tuesday, which cited a letter viewed by the publication. The price represents a 17% premium to FIGS closing price on Tuesday.

Story3 and affiliated funds own a little more than 1% stake in FIGS, according to the report.

Story3 said Fortress Investment Group, one of its strategic limited partners, is prepared to provide the debt financing for a transaction, according to the letter.

Figs has a $889 million market cap and its shares have dropped 26% this year. The scrub maker's stock has dropped 77% since going public in May 2021.
